details.publicNotes:
Conversion notes (this module)

Role: the only spirit-skulled Deadwatcher chieftain to last a full week inside the castle — risen the next night as a fext, now mistress of the guest wing (B29), served by the orc-spirit wraiths of B33. Found staring out her arrow slit, lost in thought; she won't attack until spoken to or attacked, then fights in eerie silence. Big noise in B28 brings her in 1d4 rounds. The kill: the dead orc in B7 carries a +1 striking obsidian battleaxe — the castle's one ready tool for ending her (the blood-scrawled warnings in B7/B13/B25 are her victims' work). Permanently slain, her grateful spirit sighs thanks and infuses her own axe — it becomes the sinister intelligent weapon @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-items.Item.ch5UkwarAxe00001]{Ukwar}.

description.value: These axes are designed explicitly as weapons, rather than tools. They typically weigh less, with a shaft reinforced with metal bands or bolts, and have a sharper blade, making them ideal for chopping limbs rather than wood.

With a superior design and excellent craftsmanship, this steel shield has higher Hardness than its non-magical counterparts, making it harder to break and destroy.

The shield has Hardness 13, HP 104, and BT 52.

When reduced to 0 hit points by anything other than a glass weapon or an obsidian weapon; a fext is not destroyed, but instead becomes @Compendium[pf2e.conditionitems.Unconscious]{Unconscious}. Additionally, [[/br 1d4 #minutes]]{1d4 minutes} after falling unconscious, a fext gains fast healing 5. To be completely destroyed, a fext must be reduced to 0 hit points by a glass or obsidian weapon, or once it is rendered unconscious, its head must be severed and anointed with holy water. Once destroyed, a fext dissolves into fine ash.

Requirements You can bleed.

Trigger A creature deals piercing, slashing, or persistent bleed damage to you.

You curse the target, punishing it for having the audacity to spill your blood. The target takes @Localize[PF2E.PersistentDamage.Bleed2d6.success] and must attempt a Will save.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target takes half the persistent bleed damage.

Failure The target takes the full persistent bleed damage. Until the bleeding stops, the target has weakness 1 to piercing and slashing damage. @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spell-effects.buXx8Azr4BYWPtFg]{Spell Effect: Blood Vendetta (Failure)}

Critical Failure As failure, but the target takes double the persistent bleed damage.

Heightened (+2) The persistent bleed damage increases by @Localize[PF2E.PersistentDamage.Bleed2d6.success].

You snuff the life out of a creature on the brink of death. The target must attempt a Will save. If this kills it, you gain 10 temporary HP and a +1 status bonus to attack and damage rolls for 10 minutes.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target's @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.yZRUzMqrMmfLu0V1]{Dying} value increases by 1.

Failure The target dies.

Siphoning negative energy into yourself, your hand radiates a pale darkness. Your touch weakens the living and disorients undead, possibly even causing them to flee. The effect depends on whether the target is living or undead.

  • Living Creature The spell deals negative damage equal to 1d4 plus your spellcasting modifier. The target attempts a basic Fortitude save, but is also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 1} for 1 round on a critical failure.
  • Undead Creature The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Flat-Footed} for 1 round on a failed Fortitude save. On a critical failure, the target is also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.sDPxOjQ9kx2RZE8D]{Fleeing} for 1 round unless it succeeds at a Will save.

Heightened (+1) The negative damage to living creatures increases by 1d4.

You cloud the target's mind and daze it with a mental jolt. The jolt deals mental damage equal to your spellcasting ability modifier; the target must attempt a basic Will save. If the target critically fails the save, it is also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1}.

Heightened (+2) The damage increases by 1d6.

When Ukwar damages a living creature with its claw or battleaxe Strike, Ukwar gains 12 temporary Hit Points and the creature must succeed at a @Check[type:fortitude|dc:29] save or become @Compendium[pf2e.conditionitems.Drained]{Drained 1}. Further damage dealt by Ukwar increases the amount of drain by 1 on a failed save, to a maximum of @Compendium[pf2e.conditionitems.Drained]{Drained 4}.

Requirements Ukwar's last action was a successful battleaxe or claw Strike

Effect Ukwar can channel her negative energy through her battleaxe as if she had @Compendium[pf2e.actionspf2e.Cast a Spell]{Cast a Spell}. Ukwar attempts a battleaxe Strike. On a

Success, add [[/r {1d8}[negative]]]{1d8 negative damage} to the damage. On a critical hit, add [[/r {1d8}[persistent,negative]]]{1d8} @Compendium[pf2e.conditionitems.Persistent Damage]{Persistent negative Damage} instead.
